user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Blue Yonder Airlines. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 09:14 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 13:52 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 11:14 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with territory planning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-02-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is territory planning.

[00:08:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:17:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:23:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:25:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:29:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:33:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:38:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:42:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.
